metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 16 batch 9 O
metadata.notes: Public-web research only; no external communication was made. Breeder, employer, applicant, titleholder, agent, licensee, and successor roles remain distinct. Breeder employment is not treated as personal or institutional ownership without cultivar-level evidence. Source-name errors, entity/person mismatches, mixed portfolios, and unresolved title chains are marked record_split_required and/or suppress_scoring.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Tsuyoshi Shoji of the Yamanashi Prefecture fruit-tree breeding program. The reviewed grape records are collaborative public-program breeding work with Hideo Bessho, Masanori Miyake, Koichi Koike, Mari Kondo, and other Yamanashi staff. This affiliation does not establish Shoji as a personal holder, and Yamanashi is not treated as holder outside cultivar-level registry evidence.
profiles.headquarters: Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station, 1204 Ezohara, Yamanashi-shi, Yamanashi 405-0043, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station remains the continuing institutional grape-breeding bench. No personal ownership, private spinout, or documented transfer from Shoji was found.
profiles.candidateParent: Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station / Yamanashi Prefecture
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official prefectural sources and MAFF cultivar material place Shoji's grape work in the Yamanashi public breeding program; legal title still requires cultivar- and territory-specific verification.

webResearchSources.label: Yamanashi official grape-breeding program
Yamanashi official research report
Japan MAFF Sun Semillon registration notice

profiles.webResearchNotes: Acquisition relevance is institutional, not personal. Suppress Shoji as a standalone commercial target; retain Yamanashi as the candidate rollup and verify the current applicant or holder for each cultivar and territory. No record split is indicated within the reviewed grape cluster.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to In Ha Lee (Lee In-ha / 이인하) of the Strawberry Research Institute at the Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services. Official institute material identifies the Nonsan program and its cultivar releases. Lee's breeder role is employment evidence, not proof of personal ownership; no reviewed source supports a personally held portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Strawberry Research Institute, Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services, Nonsan, Chungcheongnam-do, Republic of Korea
profiles.leadershipSummary: The strawberry work is a continuing Chungcheongnam-do public program with a multi-person institutional bench. No private succession or transfer from Lee was identified.
profiles.primaryContactName: Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services
profiles.candidateParent: Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services - Strawberry Research Institute
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official provincial publications and crop research identify Lee and the cultivars with the Nonsan Strawberry Research Institute; current legal title remains a cultivar-level question.

webResearchSources.label: Chungcheongnam-do official strawberry cultivar paper by In Ha Lee
Official institute overview and contact
Peer-reviewed Nonsan strawberry-program affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: Acquisition relevance is the provincial institute/program, not Lee personally. Suppress the individual as a commercial target and verify Korean and foreign title records per cultivar. The reviewed strawberry records form one institutional cluster; no record split is required.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Myeong Hyeon Nam (Nam Myeong-hyeon / 남명현) of the Strawberry Research Institute at the Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services. Nam appears on the same Nonsan public-program cultivar cluster as In Ha Lee and Tae Il Kim. Breeder authorship does not establish personal ownership, and no personal holding vehicle was found.
profiles.headquarters: Strawberry Research Institute, Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services, Nonsan, Chungcheongnam-do, Republic of Korea
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Nonsan strawberry work continues as a provincial research program with institutional staff and succession. No private ownership or personal transfer by Nam was identified.
profiles.primaryContactName: Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services
profiles.candidateParent: Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services - Strawberry Research Institute
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official and peer-reviewed sources identify Nam with the Nonsan Strawberry Research Institute and the relevant cultivar program; legal title must still be checked by cultivar and jurisdiction.

webResearchSources.label: KCI Maehyang cultivar paper naming Myeong Hyeon Nam
Official Chungcheongnam-do institute overview and contact
Peer-reviewed Nonsan strawberry-program affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: Acquisition relevance is the provincial institute/program, not Nam personally. Suppress the individual as a direct target and verify title per cultivar and territory. The reviewed strawberry records are one institutional cluster; no record split is required.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Dr. Encarnación Ortega Pastor of the Plant Breeding Department at CEBAS-CSIC in Murcia. The reviewed apricot and almond records belong to CEBAS-CSIC breeding collaborations. An official PBR bulletin names Spain's Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas as applicant for Primorosa and Ortega as a breeder; that evidence is cultivar-specific and is not extended to every record.
profiles.headquarters: CEBAS-CSIC, Campus Universitario de Espinardo, 30100 Murcia, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: The work sits in a continuing CEBAS-CSIC institutional fruit-breeding program with José Egea, Pedro Martínez-Gómez, Federico Dicenta, Manuel Rubio, and other collaborators. No personal holding company or private succession from Ortega was found.
profiles.candidateParent: CEBAS-CSIC / Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official applicant evidence for Primorosa and public institutional affiliation support the CSIC rollup; ownership of other cultivars must be verified individually.

webResearchSources.label: Official Morocco PBR bulletin naming CSIC applicant and Ortega breeder
ISHS CEBAS-CSIC identity and public contact
CEBAS-CSIC official site

profiles.webResearchNotes: Acquisition relevance is CSIC/CEBAS institutional licensing, not Ortega personally. The apricot and almond records can remain together as one employer program, but title must be verified per cultivar; Primorosa is the only reviewed record with explicit applicant evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: Ukrainian agricultural legal entity misclassified as breeder person
profiles.ownershipSummary: The raw string resolves to Ukrainian agricultural company STOV Enohrai (СТОВ «ЕНОГРАЙ»), not a natural-person breeder. A 2025 Ukrainian Institute bulletin lists the reviewed Hizela, Kordia 1, VSL 2.3, Early Star 1, and HF 677.1 applications under applicant code 3417 while separately naming Ruslan Tarasovych Holub as variety author. A public variety-register mirror maps Enohrai to applicant/dissemination-right roles, but the accessible official bulletin does not expose the applicant-code-to-legal-name key. Do not infer breeder authorship or full PBR ownership from the entity string.
profiles.leadershipSummary: No reliable official corporate site, management record, public contact, or ownership/succession history for STOV Enohrai was located. Ruslan Holub is the named variety author in the official filing and must not be merged into the entity record without a separate identity review.
profiles.primaryContactName: Ukrainian Institute for Plant Variety Examination
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Registry authority for record verification; not an Enohrai contact
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The raw name is itself an entity and public register material associates it with the variety records, but the official applicant code and full legal identity/contact were not conclusively joined in the reviewed public sources.

webResearchSources.label: Official Ukraine plant-variety bulletin, issue 5 of 2025
Public Ukrainian variety-register mirror for Hizela 6.1
Ukrainian Institute for Plant Variety Examination

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress scoring and split the entity/applicant record from the natural-person author record. Acquisition relevance is unresolved pending an authoritative legal-entity match for applicant code 3417, corporate ownership, contact, and cultivar-level rights. No outreach was attempted.

profiles.ownershipType: Italian public-university breeder with cultivar-specific commercial assignees/licensees
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Prof. Bruno Mezzetti of the Department of Agricultural, Food and Environmental Sciences at Università Politecnica delle Marche (UNIVPM). He is a breeder, not a blanket holder. For example, the US Romina patent names Mezzetti and Franco Capocasa as inventors but records Hargreaves Plants Ltd. as assignee; other UNIVPM cultivars are presented or licensed through Geoplant and other commercial partners. Those roles and territories must remain separate.
profiles.headquarters: Università Politecnica delle Marche, Via Brecce Bianche, 60131 Ancona, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Mezzetti is an established UNIVPM professor and program leader. UNIVPM is the employer/breeding institution, while Hargreaves, Geoplant, and other parties can hold or commercialize particular cultivar rights; no single universal successor chain was found.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official UNIVPM affiliation and breeding materials establish the employer/program rollup, but patent and licensing evidence shows that legal/commercial rights vary by cultivar and territory.

webResearchSources.label: Official UNIVPM CV and public contact
Romina US patent and Hargreaves assignment history
Geoplant Lauretta page naming Marche Polytechnic University as breeder

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress Mezzetti as a personal acquisition target. Record split is required by cultivar and jurisdiction: preserve UNIVPM as employer/breeding program, Hargreaves as the evidenced US Romina assignee, and cultivar-specific nursery/license relationships separately. Concettina is also a different crop/right chain from the strawberry cluster.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Fernando Pistón Pistón, R&D Manager at Fresas Nuevos Materiales S.A. (FNM) in Huelva. Spain's official variety bulletin distinguishes the roles for DUNA FNM: FNM is the titleholder and Pistón is the breeder. The reviewed evidence therefore supports a corporate rollup to FNM, not personal ownership by Pistón.
profiles.headquarters: Fresas Nuevos Materiales S.A., Avenida de Andalucía 70, Local Izquierda, 21007 Huelva, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: FNM is a producer- and nursery-sector breeding company; its official site describes majority ownership through the Huelva producer sector/FID together with nursery-sector participation. No personal succession or holding vehicle for Pistón was found.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: FNM identifies Pistón as R&D Manager, and Spain's official register distinguishes him as breeder from FNM as holder for DUNA FNM.

webResearchSources.label: FNM partner profile naming Fernando Pistón as R&D Manager
Spain official plant-variety bulletin for DUNA FNM
FNM official ownership and business profile

profiles.webResearchNotes: Acquisition relevance rolls to FNM, not Pistón personally. Preserve breeder and holder fields separately and verify pending selections by application number; the reviewed strawberry cluster does not require a person-level split.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to the late Dr. Eli Tomer, a fruit breeder at Israel's Agricultural Research Organization (ARO/Volcani Center). ARO project and patent sources place the mango selections in planned Volcani breeding with Uri Lavi, David Saada, Yuval Cohen, and Tomer. Patent assignee records point to the State of Israel/Ministry of Agriculture/ARO, not Tomer personally.
profiles.headquarters: Agricultural Research Organization, Volcani Center, Rishon LeZion, Israel
profiles.leadershipSummary: Tomer is deceased. The institutional breeding and commercialization interface continues through ARO/Volcani and its Kidum business unit; surviving co-breeders and current program staff represent scientific continuity, not automatic personal succession to title.
profiles.candidateParent: State of Israel, Ministry of Agriculture - Agricultural Research Organization
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official ARO program material and patent assignee records support the public-institute rollup; each jurisdiction's current title and status still require verification.

webResearchSources.label: Official ARO project page identifying the late Eli Tomer
Noa mango patent naming the Volcani breeding team
Official ARO Kidum licensing contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a personal target because Tomer is deceased and the work is governmental. Acquisition relevance is an institutional licensing inquiry to ARO/Kidum after confirming current title and expiration status cultivar by cultivar.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Dr. Markus Kellerhals, the long-serving Agroscope apple breeder who retired in 2022 and later served as a FRUCTUS co-president. The Milwa patent records Agroscope Changins-Wädenswil as assignee, not Kellerhals. Agroscope owns the public breeding program, while VariCom commercializes selected Agroscope fruit varieties and coordinates licensees; commercialization is not itself proof that VariCom owns each plant right.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Kellerhals has retired from Agroscope. The current Agroscope fruit-breeding team is the scientific successor, and VariCom GmbH is the continuing commercialization interface for selected program varieties. Cultivar-level ownership and exclusive regional marketing arrangements remain separate.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official Agroscope and VariCom sources place the reviewed apple portfolio in the Swiss federal breeding program, and Milwa patent records provide cultivar-specific Agroscope assignment evidence.

webResearchSources.label: Official Agroscope apple-breeding program
Milwa patent and Agroscope assignment
VariCom official role and contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress Kellerhals as a personal acquisition target. Use Agroscope as the institutional parent and VariCom as a public commercialization contact, while verifying title, trademark, club/marketing, and license rights separately for each cultivar and territory.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Dr. Vered Irihimovitch, research scientist at ARO/Volcani and project manager of the official avocado breeding project. The Tami and Miriam US patents name Irihimovitch among the inventors and record the State of Israel/Ministry of Agriculture/ARO as applicant or assignee. Her breeder and project-manager roles do not create personal ownership.
profiles.headquarters: Agricultural Research Organization, Volcani Center, Rishon LeZion, Israel
profiles.leadershipSummary: Irihimovitch is the current public-program project manager. ARO/Volcani and Kidum provide institutional scientific and commercialization continuity; no private holding company or personal succession was found.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Research Scientist and Avocado Breeding Project Manager
profiles.candidateParent: State of Israel, Ministry of Agriculture - Agricultural Research Organization
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official ARO identity/project pages and cultivar patent assignments support the public-institute rollup, subject to current-title checks in each jurisdiction.

webResearchSources.label: Official ARO Vered Irihimovitch profile and contact
Official ARO avocado breeding project
Tami avocado patent and State of Israel assignment

profiles.webResearchNotes: Acquisition relevance is institutional licensing through ARO/Kidum, not Irihimovitch personally. No record split is indicated for the reviewed avocado cluster, but title and status must be confirmed per cultivar and territory.

profiles.ownershipType: retired UK institutional breeder with mixed owner chains and one unrelated homonym record
profiles.ownershipSummary: The strawberry cluster resolves to Dr. David William Simpson, the East Malling/NIAB EMR strawberry breeder who retired in 2018. He is not the holder. Rights split by cultivar and time: East Malling Research and later NIAB appear in assignment histories, Bayer acquired NIAB's strawberry breeding program and Malling Fruits portfolio effective 2024, and Buddy has a separate Hargreaves Plants Ltd. chain. The aggregated avocado record is unrelated to this UK strawberry breeder and must be split as a homonym.
profiles.headquarters: Historical program: East Malling, Kent, United Kingdom; current acquired strawberry program: Bayer Crop Science
profiles.leadershipSummary: Simpson retired in 2018. NIAB's program later moved to Bayer, which became the current owner/operator of the acquired breeding program and Malling Fruits portfolio in 2024. That transaction does not absorb cultivars with separate holder chains such as Buddy, and it does not resolve the unrelated avocado row.
profiles.candidateParent: NIAB EMR (historical employer); Bayer Crop Science (acquired program/portfolio)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Identity and employment are strong for the strawberry records, and NIAB documents the 2024 program/portfolio sale to Bayer. Cultivar-specific assignment histories show exceptions, and the avocado record is a separate person.

webResearchSources.label: NIAB official announcement of strawberry-program sale to Bayer
Mayflower patent assignment history
Buddy patent and Hargreaves assignment

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress aggregate scoring. Split the unrelated avocado row from David William Simpson's strawberry records, then split the strawberry portfolio by cultivar and current jurisdictional holder. Simpson is retired and not a personal acquisition target; Bayer is relevant only for the acquired NIAB/Malling portfolio, while Hargreaves and other evidenced holders remain separate.

profiles.ownershipType: source-name error for US public-university breeding employee
profiles.ownershipSummary: The requested string is a source error. The exact person is Rebecca L. McCluskey of Oregon State University's hazelnut breeding program, not 'McCuskey.' OSU sources identify McCluskey as program staff and co-author/co-breeder with Shawn Mehlenbacher and David C. Smith. OSU controls and licenses university plant IP under its policies; no evidence supports personal ownership by McCluskey.
profiles.headquarters: Oregon State University, Department of Horticulture, Corvallis, Oregon 97331, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: McCluskey works within OSU's continuing hazelnut breeding program led by Shawn Mehlenbacher. Her staff role and breeder credit are distinct from OSU's institutional licensing role; no personal succession vehicle was found.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The exact reviewed cultivar cluster and official OSU pages resolve the malformed name to Rebecca L. McCluskey and place the work in OSU's public breeding and licensing program.

webResearchSources.label: Official OSU Rebecca McCluskey profile and contact
Official OSU Pacific Northwest hazelnut cultivar guide
Official OSU intellectual-property policy

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress the misspelled profile from scoring and normalize the source record to Rebecca L. McCluskey; do not create a second person. Acquisition relevance is OSU institutional licensing, not McCluskey personally. The eight hazelnut records belong to the same OSU cluster after correction.

profiles.ownershipType: source-name error for US public-university breeding employee
profiles.ownershipSummary: The requested string is a source error. The exact person is David C. Smith of Oregon State University's hazelnut breeding program, not 'Smnit.' His official OSU profile identifies him as technical support working for Shawn Mehlenbacher and assisting cultivar development, and it lists publications as Smith, D. C. with Rebecca L. McCluskey. OSU's institutional IP and licensing role is distinct from Smith's breeder credit.
profiles.headquarters: Oregon State University, Department of Horticulture, 2750 SW Campus Way, Corvallis, Oregon 97331, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Smith works in OSU's continuing hazelnut breeding team under Shawn Mehlenbacher. No personal holding entity or ownership succession was found.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Technical Support, OSU hazelnut breeding program
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The identical cultivar cluster and official OSU profile resolve the malformed source name to David C. Smith and place his work in OSU's breeding program.

webResearchSources.label: Official OSU David Smith profile and contact
Official OSU Pacific Northwest hazelnut cultivar guide
Official OSU hazelnut breeding program

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress the malformed profile from scoring and normalize the source record to David C. Smith; do not create a second person. Acquisition relevance is OSU institutional licensing, not Smith personally. The eight hazelnut records belong to the same OSU cluster after correction.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to the late Dr. Don McKenzie, a New Zealand apple breeder who died in 1988. Official Canadian PBR material for Sciglo/Southern Snap names McKenzie and Allan White as breeders and The Horticulture and Food Research Institute of New Zealand Ltd. (HortResearch) as holder. That is institutional, cultivar-specific title evidence, not personal ownership by McKenzie.
profiles.headquarters: Historical holder: HortResearch, New Zealand; current institutional successor interface: Plant & Food Research, a division of the Bioeconomy Science Institute
profiles.leadershipSummary: HortResearch merged with Crop & Food Research in 2008 to form Plant & Food Research. Since 1 July 2025, Plant & Food Research has operated as a division of New Zealand's Bioeconomy Science Institute. This institutional succession is relevant for diligence contact but is not treated as automatic proof of current cultivar title without assignment and status records.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Current institutional IP and licensing interface
profiles.candidateParent: HortResearch (historical holder); Plant & Food Research / Bioeconomy Science Institute (institutional successor interface)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official PBR evidence identifies HortResearch as holder for Sciglo, and New Zealand government/institutional sources document the later organizational succession; current title requires a cultivar-level chain check.

webResearchSources.label: Official Canadian PBR record for Sciglo / Southern Snap
New Zealand government announcement of the HortResearch merger
Plant & Food Research official history

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress McKenzie as a personal target because he is deceased and the rights were institutional. Acquisition relevance is limited to current institutional title/licensing diligence through Plant & Food Research/Bioeconomy Science Institute. Verify each cultivar's assignment, expiration, and territory; do not infer current title solely from organizational succession.

profiles.ownershipType: Bulgarian public-research institute breeder and director
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolves to Assoc. Prof. Galina Yordanova Dyakova, PhD, director of the Institute of Agriculture and Seed Science 'Obraztsov Chiflik' - Rousse, a scientific unit of Bulgaria's Agricultural Academy. The institute's official site presents the grape portfolio as its own varieties and identifies Dyakova's program role, but no reviewed authoritative PBR record established the current legal holder for each cultivar. Employment and institutional branding are therefore not treated as title.
profiles.headquarters: Institute of Agriculture and Seed Science 'Obraztsov Chiflik' - Rousse, 1 Prof. Ivan Ivanov Street, 7007 Rousse, Bulgaria
profiles.leadershipSummary: Dyakova is the institute's current director and an active vine researcher. The institute and Agricultural Academy provide institutional continuity; no separate private owner, assignment chain, or cultivar-level legal succession was verified.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Director, Institute of Agriculture and Seed Science - Rousse
profiles.candidateParent: Institute of Agriculture and Seed Science 'Obraztsov Chiflik' - Rousse / Agricultural Academy of Bulgaria
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official institute pages establish Dyakova's identity, directorship, and grape-program affiliation but do not prove the registered holder for every cultivar.

webResearchSources.label: Official Galina Dyakova profile
Official institute structure and director contact
Official institute vine program and varieties

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress scoring until an authoritative cultivar-level holder record is obtained. Dyakova is not a personal acquisition target; the institute/Agricultural Academy is the affiliation rollup only. No record split is indicated within the Bulgarian grape cluster, but legal ownership and current right status remain unresolved.
